source: Hand-curated links to iconic baseball moments. Using YouTube search URLs (not specific video IDs) so the broader catalog surfaces — avoids dead links if a specific upload is removed.
note: Add ~5-10 new entries per week. Each entry describes a historically significant moment and links to a YouTube search curated to surface the best available footage.

moments.title: Jackie Robinson breaks the MLB color barrier
moments.description: Robinson's Brooklyn debut at Ebbets Field. Opening Day of a transformed league. He went 0-for-3 but scored the winning run in a 5-3 Dodgers victory.

moments.title: Willie Mays's over-the-shoulder catch
moments.description: Game 1 of the 1954 World Series. Mays tracked Vic Wertz's 420-foot drive with his back to home plate, caught it over his shoulder, and spun to fire the ball back to the infield — often called the greatest defensive play in baseball history.

moments.title: Roger Maris breaks Babe Ruth's single-season home run record
moments.description: Maris hit his 61st HR of the season off Tracy Stallard at Yankee Stadium on the final day of the season, breaking the 34-year-old record set by Babe Ruth.

moments.title: Hank Aaron's 715th home run breaks Babe Ruth's career record
moments.description: Aaron hit career HR #715 off Al Downing at Atlanta-Fulton County Stadium, passing Babe Ruth's career record of 714. Two fans jumped on the field to run the bases alongside him.

moments.title: Carlton Fisk waves his HR fair — 1975 World Series Game 6
moments.description: 12th inning at Fenway. Fisk lined one down the left-field line and famously waved it fair with his arms — the iconic image of one of the greatest World Series games ever played.

moments.title: Kirk Gibson's injured-leg walk-off home run
moments.description: Game 1 of the 1988 World Series. Injured and unable to play the field, Gibson limped to the plate as a pinch-hitter in the bottom of the 9th and hit a two-run walk-off home run off Dennis Eckersley. Vin Scully's call — 'In a year that has been so improbable, the impossible has happened' — is itself legendary.

moments.title: Cal Ripken Jr. breaks Lou Gehrig's consecutive games record
moments.description: Ripken played his 2,131st consecutive game at Camden Yards, passing Lou Gehrig's 56-year-old record. The game paused for a 22-minute standing ovation in the middle of the 5th inning after the game became official.

moments.title: Mark McGwire breaks Roger Maris's single-season HR record
moments.description: McGwire hit his 62nd HR of the season off Steve Trachsel at Busch Stadium, passing Maris's 37-year-old mark. Sosa crossed the diamond to embrace him after the homer. McGwire finished the season with 70.

moments.title: Cubs end 108-year championship drought
moments.description: Game 7 of the 2016 World Series. A 17-minute rain delay before the 10th inning. Cubs won 8-7 in extras. Their first championship since 1908.

moments.title: The perfect game that wasn't (Galarraga / Jim Joyce)
moments.description: Armando Galarraga lost a perfect game on the 27th out when umpire Jim Joyce called Jason Donald safe at first. Joyce apologized to Galarraga in the clubhouse after the game; Galarraga presented the lineup card the next day in a public reconciliation. The incident led to expanded instant replay in MLB.
